methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Curio Solutions is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Ed McGinnis and Pete Zweifel, having raised $16M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a nuclear energy sector universe of 3 public comps (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, and General Matter.

Stage-progression methodology proves applicable here, using the $16M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to later stages, yielding $32M. Peer-median analysis was not applicable given insufficient narrow-stage comparable data within the nuclear energy sector; while 4 same-sector peers exist, none of the filtered subsets (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier) returned the minimum 3 datapoints required for reliable median benchmarking. Relying on all-sector medians would introduce material distortion.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to single-method support, indicates a valuation range of $27M (low) to $37M (high), with a median of $32M. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight-peer revenue or margin comparables at Series A stage within nuclear energy, and the nascent, heterogeneous nature of the sector limiting confidence in stage-progression multipliers. The $32M median should be treated as a directional anchor pending access to superior peer financials or demonstrated technical/commercial milestones.
